user_stories.goal: Generate a personalised day-by-day city travel itinerary from a web UI or API by providing destination, trip length, budget and interests
user_stories.benefit: Receive a clear, varied, and budget-aware plan to make trip planning faster and reduce decision fatigue
acceptance_criteria: User can submit the planner form or POST to /api/itinerary with fields: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style
Successful response (HTTP 200) is JSON containing top-level keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, overview, itinerary, tips
The 'itinerary' value is a list with length exactly equal to the submitted days
Each itinerary day object includes keys: day (number), morning (activity or list), afternoon (activity or list), evening (activity or list), budget_note (string)
Estimated cost values in activity entries are numeric and adjusted by the selected budget level (not textual labels)
Activity titles and descriptions are human-readable strings (no raw dictionaries or '[object Object]')
Invalid or missing input returns HTTP 400 with a JSON error object describing the problem

user_stories.goal: Implement a stable Flask API that reliably generates itineraries following the coursework quality contract
user_stories.benefit: Provide a deterministic, testable API for the frontend and automated tests to consume
acceptance_criteria: API exposes GET /health returning HTTP 200 and a small JSON health object
API exposes POST /api/itinerary that accepts JSON body with keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style
POST /api/itinerary validates inputs and returns HTTP 400 with JSON error on invalid input
A successful POST returns a JSON object containing the required top-level keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, overview, itinerary, tips

Itinerary generation expands each interest into multiple concrete activities (e.g., food -> street breakfast, market tasting, signature restaurant, dessert stop, evening snack) before assembling daily plans
Activities are rotated across morning/afternoon/evening using destination, day number, time slot and user interests so the same generic fallback does not repeat daily
Each day is assigned a different theme or planning focus where possible (e.g., culture day, food day, outdoor day)
Activity entries may be JSON objects but include plain string fields for title and description and numeric estimated_cost so frontends never render '[object Object]'

user_stories.goal: Build a deterministic website interface that collects inputs, calls the Flask API, and renders readable itineraries using stable IDs and functions
user_stories.benefit: Deliver a testable UI that integrates cleanly with automated checks and later image handling components
acceptance_criteria: The frontend form uses stable element IDs: plannerForm, destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, resultsContainer, daysContainer, formMessage, errorMessage
The frontend implements stable functions: collectFormData, validateFormData, setLoading, showError, renderItinerary, renderDay, renderActivity, formatActivityText
Form submission calls POST /api/itinerary and handles HTTP 200 and HTTP 400 JSON responses; showError displays JSON error details in errorMessage
The UI renders each itinerary day with day number, morning, afternoon, evening, and budget_note using renderDay and renderActivity, and never shows raw object text like '[object Object]'
The frontend does not create its own image preview area or attempt to manage images (image integration is handled later by a separate agent)
Rendered estimated costs are numeric values displayed clearly (no low/medium/high strings)

user_stories.goal: Ensure deterministic outputs and contract compliance for automated pytest and Docker-based checks
user_stories.benefit: Automated tests can verify API, frontend IDs/functions, and itinerary structure reliably across runs
acceptance_criteria: Automated tests can call GET /health and POST /api/itinerary and assert required top-level keys and types
Tests verify that itinerary list length equals requested days and that each day object contains day, morning, afternoon, evening, and budget_note
Tests assert estimated_cost fields are numeric and budgets influence cost values deterministically
Tests confirm interest expansion: a multi-day trip shows varied concrete activities derived from each interest rather than repeating the same three items
Tests confirm activity rotation so morning/afternoon/evening items vary by day using destination and day number, and that each day has a distinct theme where possible
Tests assert frontend exposes stable IDs and functions and that rendered activity text is human-readable with no '[object Object]' output
